mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical correlation with FEDFUNDS changes (Rank IC < 0.02) across all tested periods.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that immediately trigger negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE rises above the 0.2pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand expansion, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day returns (win rate < 50%) in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices indicate strengthening manufacturing output that supports corporate earnings growth, yielding positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days during late-cycle expansions.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases produce negative or flat 20-day forward returns during late-cycle expansion periods, with a Rank IC below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly inflation readings above 0.4% force markets to price in aggressive monetary tightening, causing immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the following 5 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL readings above 0.4% are followed by positive 5-day returns in over 60% of historical observations.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in total nonfarm payrolls and decreases in the unemployment rate confirm robust labor market tightening that sustains positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined PAYEMS and UNRATE signal yields a negative 60-day return win rate below 45% across recovery periods.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP reflects expanding economic output that directly boosts corporate profit expectations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show zero predictive power for 20-day forward returns, with a rolling Rank IC consistently below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle signals supply-side contraction that outweighs demand factors, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening cycles are followed by positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of cases.
